name: Duck and Cover
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------STOCK-----------------------------------
Giblets from the duck (remove neck)
1/4 cup clarified butter
3 medium mushrooms, sliced
1 medium onion, diced
1 carrot, diced
1 celery stalk, diced
1 bay leaf
1 teaspoon sage
1 cup white wine
2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar
Salt and black pepper to taste
-----------------------------------SAUCE-----------------------------------
2 tablespoons butter
2 tablespoons flour
1 cup apple juice
1 tablespoon grated orange peel
2 tablespoons triple sec
---------------------------------VEGETABLES---------------------------------
1 cup broccoli florets
1 leek, sliced thinly
1 teaspoon ground ginger
1 cup diced celery
1/2 cup diced red bell peppers
Bake and debone the duck; set aside crispy skin for pie crust.
While roasting the duck, prepare the sauce: brown giblets (except neck) in clarified butter. Add mushrooms, onion, carrot, and celery until onions are translucent. Add enough water to cover, then wine, vinegar, bay leaf, sage, salt, and pepper. Simmer 30 minutes, reduce to one cup, remove neck and bay leaf.
Make a roux with flour and butter; cook until medium brown. Add giblet stock, orange peel, triple sec, and apple juice. Cook until sauce is medium thick.
Place bottom crust in a pie dish. Arrange meat, broccoli, leeks, celery, peppers, and ginger. Pour sauce over all. Roll crispy duck skin into top crust, seal edges, and bake at 425°F for 35-40 minutes or until crust browns. Enjoy this hot, comforting entree.
